title: sambucus sibirica subsp. miquelii
intro: Sambucus sibirica subsp. miquelii, also known as the Japanese elderberry, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Adoxaceae, native to East Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Sambucus sibirica subsp. miquelii include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, as well as its potential to exhibit antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Sambucus sibirica subsp. miquelii is typically prepared for herbal use as a tea, tincture, or syrup, and can be consumed fresh or dried to harness its medicinal properties.
